item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -1 because we didn't get to know how the hell did it happen that his self worth was exteriorized, without his knowledge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah this hits hard. The thought looping, the emotional exploitation, there's parallels here to things I can relate to. I'm glad you decided to post this.

Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: in some canons and time periods, the Foundation gets much more loose with their rules on utilizing anomalies. Even though this is just an excuse, you could say that this takes place in the world and timeframe in which most of the authors other scips are written, where the Foundation is more than willing to use a permanent psychological weapon against the threats it faces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's weird and vaguely interesting, but it's just a bit too…I don't know, random I guess? I also have a kneejerk about Thaumiel SCPs, I feel like something needs to be really special to earn the designation, for the Foundation to be willing to break pretty much their core rule. This just doesn't seem special enough to me to qualify.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sort of reminds me of Marvin wielding the Point-of-View gun in Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely excellent, in terms of both concept and imagery and resisting the urge to take either to extremes. It feels raw and honest in a very refreshing way. Upvoted with gusto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So they are intentionally keeping the researcher in a containment that makes them continuously question their self-worth so the Foundation can then weaponize it? It makes sense. If you can't combat something powerful, convince it that it isn't actually powerful. Cool take on the ethical lows to which the Foundation will stoop to utilize a thaumiel class anomaly. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: not sure what to say for this. partially based on personal experiences, partially not. coldposted.
